description: Positive cases: prompts where a multi-service feature has shipped and the user wants worktrees torn down and the workspace archived. Negative cases: single-branch delete, mid-flight feature work, plain review or merge requests.

positive_cases.prompt: Clean up this feature. All branches are merged.
Tear down the worktrees for PROJ-1234.
Archive PROJ-5678. The feature shipped last week.
All three service branches are merged into the baseline. Remove the workspace for PROJ-9000.
PROJ-2000 is fully shipped. Archive the contracts and implementation plans, then clean up.
feature-state.json shows phase: cleanup and all merges populated for PROJ-1234. Continue.

negative_cases.prompt: Merge the branches for PROJ-1234. Review is approved.
negative_cases.reason_for_excluding: Branch merging is multi-branch-merge, not feature-cleanup.

negative_cases.prompt: Delete this branch — I don't need it anymore.
negative_cases.reason_for_excluding: Single-branch delete is finishing-a-feature-branch (discard disposition), not feature-level teardown.

negative_cases.prompt: Review the contracts for PROJ-1234 before we open PRs.
The integration test failed — clean up the test data so we can rerun.
negative_cases.reason_for_excluding: Test data cleanup, not feature workspace teardown.

negative_cases.prompt: Clean up the feature for PROJ-3333 — we still have one branch un-merged but lets not wait.
negative_cases.reason_for_excluding: Router correctly declines when the user states branches are un-merged — the skill description requires all branches to be merged first. The assistant handles the inline refusal without invoking feature-cleanup. A prompt that omits the un-merged qualifier would route to the skill and then be refused internally.

negative_cases.prompt: Ship this feature — all service branches need to be merged now.
negative_cases.reason_for_excluding: Shipping (merging) is multi-branch-merge. Cleanup happens after that step.
